text:
Professor: Steffi Wulff | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4721 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Amazing lectures, Clear grading criteria, Caring
Dr Steffi was the most caring professor I've had so far and her class is very engaging. I love her lecture style and always enjoyed going to her class. I just wish she taught more courses here at UF.

Professor: Steffi Wulff | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4721 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Amazing lectures, Caring, Respected
Dr. Wulff is probably one of my favorite professors I have had at UF. She is so passionate about her course and it is clear she really cares about her students. Attendance is mandatory but class is so interesting it almost doesn't feel like class at all. I recommend Dr. Wulff very highly!

Professor: Steffi Wulff | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: L101 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: A+ | Tags: none
She is really super-friendly and full of skills of a teacher, which many professors at the department of linguistics lack; unfortunately. You feel that she understands you well and tries really hard to be engaged in the research along with you. She understands that there are some psychological barriers in learning and that people are not machines.

Professor: Steffi Wulff | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4390 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: A | Tags: none
Dr. Wulff is an incredible professor. She is very passionate about her field of study and it's a very infectious attitude! She is also very personable and easy to talk to. If you are looking for a good introduction to the study of corpus linguistics, the class that Dr. Wulff teaches is a must-take.

Professor: Steffi Wulff | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN3010 | Quality: 4/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: N/A | Tags: none
Steffi is incredibly passionaterelatable and makes this intro class so engaging for students. I really appreciated her enthusiasm for the subject and I can't wait to take another class of hers.

Professor: Edith Kaan | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4790 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: none
I thought Professor Kaan was great. I took her course on Brain and Language and learned a ton about neurolinguistics. She is very organized and did a very good job covering the subject.  Her workload is a bit busy but nothing impossible to handle. Otherwise I'd highly recommend this class

Professor: Edith Kaan | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4790 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Amazing lectures, Gives good feedback, Respected
Took Brain and Language with Dr. Kaan, and she's awesome. She doesn't grade harshly, but she does give meaningful feedback. She is intelligent and really knowledgeable, and her course is well organized. I learned way more in this class than I did in any of my core classes this semester. She even keeps it fun occasionally with games or activities.

Professor: Edith Kaan | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4930 | Quality: 2/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: A- | Tags: Clear grading criteria
I took Dr. Kaan's Stats class last semester. Although she is definitely someone who knows the material very well, there were some examples that she provided in class that made me uncomfortable. Definitely take her classes, you'll learn a lot but also expect to see some awkward interactions.

Professor: Edith Kaan | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4930 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: N/A | Tags: Lots of homework, Lecture heavy, EXTRA CREDIT
I thought Dr. Kaan was great! I took her Statistics for Linguists class and really liked it! She's really smart and teaches well too. Material can be kinda tricky as the course progresses but as long as you stay up with the reading and assignments you'll be fine and will learn a lot! Highly recommend taking her!

Professor: Edith Kaan | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN6707 | Quality: 3/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: A | Tags: Lecture heavy, Tough grader
Mediocre course and lectures. Long and boring Powerpoint presentations that seem to never end. It seems that Professor Kaan recycles her Powerpoints semester after semester without even spending time to review them before class.

Professor: Imanol Suarez-Palma | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN3700 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: EXTRA CREDIT, Amazing lectures, Hilarious
AMAZING lecturer!! He taught mostly in spanish but would always break it down in english if necessary; the seminar practice was helpful, easy exams with study guides that mirror the exam, almost daily true or false quizzes with two attempts, plus he gives 2% grade boost for participating in linguistic research studies! LOVED this class and topic!

Professor: Imanol Suarez-Palma | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN4850 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Amazing lectures, Hilarious, Graded by few things
Imanol is very passionate about linguistics and adds humor sometimes. Some of the syntax concepts like x-bar trees or "se" sentences can be confusing, but he offers a lot of practice in class. The exams are like his reviews and homework. If you took SPN3700 with him, you basically relearn half of the content you learned in SPN3700. Lo mejor!

Professor: Imanol Suarez-Palma | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN4850 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 4/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Hilarious, Caring, Accessible outside class
Amazing! Best Spanish professor I've had at UF. Highly recommended.

Professor: Imanol Suarez-Palma | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN4850 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 4/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Lots of homework, Hilarious, Caring
Great class overall. Course material can get complicated at times but Imanol does a great job at explaining it, both in Spanish and in English, which helps a lot. Don't miss class, ask questions and do the practice exercises in class because they're similar to what you will find in the tests. He's also really helpful during office hours.

Professor: Imanol Suarez-Palma | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN4930 | Quality: 2/5 | Difficulty: 5/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: A | Tags: Lots of homework
incredibly difficult. He has a hard time explaining the material in a way that students can understand, especially given the complexity of the topic. Lots of homework & he's not clear on what he asks for/what we're expected to turn in. When asking for additional help he's not very helpful either, can't seem to find other ways to explain things.

Professor: David Pharies | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN4780 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 4/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Hilarious, Test heavy, Graded by few things
Dr. Pharies is by far my favorite professor in the Spanish department at UF. I've taken both translation and phonetics with him. Super funny guy. Attendance not mandatory but if you skip you will fail. Always happy to help in office hours. Really intelligent, you can tell he loves what he does. Graded by only a few tests, so you must actually try.

Professor: David Pharies | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN3451 | Quality: 1/5 | Difficulty: 5/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: B | Tags: Tough grader, Test heavy, Graded by few things
Dr. Pharies has been the first professor I have ever disliked in the spanish department. His course is horribly structured, graded by 8 exams, and he has no rubric he grades based off of. He only respects Castellano spanish and nothing else. Get ready to lose your A after exam 1. He grades based off of if something sounds "good"...

Professor: David Pharies | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN3451 | Quality: 2/5 | Difficulty: 4/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: A | Tags: Test heavy, Graded by few things
Graded only by 8 translation quizzes and a final. Gives you 100+ sentences to memorize a day or 2 before the quiz. Have to memorize translations exactly word for word or you will get points deducted. Didn't really learn how to translate, more focused on memorizing too many sentences for a quiz. Easy if you can memorize word for word.

Professor: Hannah Treadway | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN2340 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Inspirational, Hilarious, Accessible outside class
One of the most amazing graduate professors here at UF. She was beyond caring and so passionate about linguistics. I have never met another teacher who was so eager to really get to know her students and connect with them on such a deeper level. She isn't teaching anymore but wherever she goes or whenever she teaches again, take her class.

Professor: Hannah Treadway | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN2340 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Get ready to read, Gives good feedback, Caring
Professora Treadway was my favorite teacher this semester. It was obvious how much she cared for us and I have become such a better spanish speaker/writer bc of this class. Never dreaded going to her class.

Professor: Hannah Treadway | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN2340 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Get ready to read, Hilarious, Caring
Shes not teaching SPN2340 again but whatever class she teaches, take that one for sure.

Professor: Hannah Treadway | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN3700 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: EXTRA CREDIT, Gives good feedback, Caring
LOVE HER SO MUCH - she is really helpful in learning linguistics associated with the spanish language, she always had great powerpoints to summarize the topics in our class and would give us practice scenarios to prepare for exams, she was also a generous grader for the more difficult homework assignments and really cares for her students!

Professor: Hannah Treadway | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: SPN1130 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Clear grading criteria, Gives good feedback, Caring
She is so sweet and her class was both fun and engaging. I thought this class would be harder but she was lenient with the grading and the LinguaMeetings were easy too. She is really nice and helps with anything you need. The online assignments are tedious but not too bad because you get multiple tries. Def recommend this professor for SPN1131!

Professor: Ethan Kutlu | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LING3020 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A- | Tags: Inspirational, Hilarious, Caring
This man is not your regular university professor; he is a rare gem. He taught us how not to categorize and label people over an entire semester. At the end of the course, he shared why he teaches this class, and i'm still recovering from that moment. Professor Kutlu, thank you for turning your grief into the most valuable class I have taken.

Professor: Ethan Kutlu | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN3010 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Hilarious, Caring, Respected
He's my prof in LING:3001 at UIowa and BY FAR the best prof I've had so far. He is very caring for his students and is great at explaining concepts and answering difficult questions that students have. He is very funny, forgiving with late work, and sometimes doesn't take attendance on purpose:) I will honestly be sad to not be in his class anymore

Professor: Ethan Kutlu | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LING3020 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A- | Tags: Amazing lectures, Inspirational, Hilarious
Where should I begin... I think he is by far the best professor I have had at UIowa. He cares about his students. He makes very challenging theoretical and abstract concepts very easy to understand. I feel lucky to be able to take his class. Also, he is hilarious and probably the kindest person alive. Just take his classes.

Professor: Ethan Kutlu | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LING3020 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Amazing lectures, Hilarious
He was a fantastic teacher. Genuinely recommend taking his classes just for him alone. He's very forgiving on assignment due dates if you need extensions. Easily one of my favorite professors!

Professor: Ethan Kutlu | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN3010 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Inspirational, Amazing lectures, Caring
I had Professor Kutlu for Spring 2020 and I cannot say enough good things about him. It is such a shame that he no longer works for UF. He was the BEST professor I have ever had. He made every class period an absolute delight. He truly cared about his students, and I will always remember the impact he had on me as a linguist and as a person.

Professor: Paula Golombek | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: IDS2935 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: none
Loves what she does! Memorized all 40+ student names in 2nd class. Super easy and chill quest class. Super chill grader. Class is discussion based - and can sometimes be boring - but she doesn't grade on participation. Take Speaking American!

Professor: Paula Golombek | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: IDS2935 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Inspirational, Caring
Super chill quest class. She can be a little passive aggressive at times but is really sweet and truly cares about her students. I personally didnt love the actual course content but wayyyyy better than other quests from what ive heard and she tries to make it interesting.

Professor: Paula Golombek | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: TSL3378 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Amazing lectures, Hilarious, Caring
One of the best teachers I have had here at UF, she is kind and understanding. Make sure you communicate with her and particiapte in class.

Professor: Paula Golombek | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: IDS2935 | Quality: 4/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: N/A | Tags: Gives good feedback, Inspirational, Caring
Dr. Golombek is incredibly caring. She took the time to learn 60 people's names and comments on assignments from a place of curiosity rather than scrutiny. Easy grader since she cares more about growth and understanding rather than correctness. Definitely better than other quest classes I've heard of. Valuable class for all students.

Professor: Paula Golombek | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: IDS2935 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Caring
Great professor, very understanding and passionate about the linguistics field. The course is easy to excel in, and she gives ample class time to workshop papers and ask questions about assignments. Participate in the lectures!!

Professor: Eleonora Rossi | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN1140 | Quality: 2/5 | Difficulty: 3/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: B | Tags: none
One of those interdisciplinary courses with no clear objective and seeming to be completely unrelated to the department that it's technically in, because this is basically just a weird and confused introductory psychology course. All of the recorded lectures (which were recorded by someone other than the instructor) are incredibly boring to watch.

Professor: Eleonora Rossi | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN1140 | Quality: 3/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Get ready to read, Lecture heavy, Online Savvy
I have never contacted Dr Rossi, as this is an online quest 1 class that is pretty easy. Lectures for everything, readings seem mandatory but lectures cover pretty much everything. Grading is awful, sometimes It would be almost 2 months before grades were uploaded. However, work is easy. Tests utilize honor lock, but you can have notes to use.

Professor: Eleonora Rossi | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN1140 | Quality: 1/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: Not sure yet | Tags: Clear grading criteria, Lots of homework, Lecture heavy
Dr. Rossi is completely unreachable. She does not respond to emails. She does not hold office hours. The TAs have expressed difficulty with getting a hold of her. The class is really easy, with video lectures (that aren't hers) but is assignment heavy, but not unmanageable. The class is a great experience, I just wish the professor was engaged.

Professor: Eleonora Rossi | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4701 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Inspirational, Graded by few things, Caring
Dr. Rossi is a great professor who cares about what she teaches. This class was easy to digest, interesting, and an easy A if you pay attention. 100% would take again!

Professor: Eleonora Rossi | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN4790 | Quality: 4/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Graded by few things
Dr. Rossi is very kind and considerate! Tests are easy if you read the textbook--especially considering they are open-book! As long as you give the class an honest effort, you will do well! Classes can be a bit boring at times, however.

Professor: Alexandrine Dunlap | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN3460 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Gives good feedback, Caring
Alex taught in a way that was interesting and made the content approachable while also emphasizing that there is always more to learn and revise when it comes to the content. She is super caring and wants everyone to succeed.

Professor: Alexandrine Dunlap | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN3460 | Quality: 4/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: Participation matters, Lots of homework, Caring
She is genuinely very caring and wants you succeed in this class. She also provides very interesting case examples of languages you would otherwise not have much exposure to which makes the class fun. Only down is a bit of homework which can take some time to do, 3 part project which is interesting, and the exams are take home. Not bad. Take Alex!!

Professor: Alexandrine Dunlap | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN3460 | Quality: 3/5 | Difficulty: 1/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: none
I feel like this class could have been more interesting. A lot of times when I would ask questions she would just say "I don't know". Attendance isn't required, but if you don't go, it will catch up with you eventually and those homework assignments will be rough. She is super sweet and chill, though.

Professor: Jamie Garner | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN2011 | Quality: 4/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: Clear grading criteria, Lecture heavy, Graded by few things
A very well-designed course for introductory linguistics. The recorded lectures cover a lot of material but they are easy to follow and good for note-taking. An inhuman amount of memorization is called for, but luckily the quizzes are short and open notes are allowed for the midterm and final. Good for anyone to take, not just freshman ling majors.

Professor: Jamie Garner | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN2011 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 4/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A | Tags: EXTRA CREDIT, Amazing lectures
Loved her, she is so sweet and has an obvious passion for linguistics. Her tests are hard, but multiple choice. Lots of extra credit, so do the extra credit and then cram before tests and you'll be fine.

Professor: Jamie Garner | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN2011 | Quality: 4/5 | Difficulty: 2/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: A+ | Tags: none
Decently interesting. Grade is pretty much entirely mid term and final. First module was weirdly hard compared to the rest of the class so don't get scared too quick. Lectures are super helpful. Use flashcard for vocab and class is mostly memorization. Was hoping to get more out of it than I did. MAXIMUM 2 hours of work a week usually much less.

Professor: Jamie Garner | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN3010 | Quality: 1/5 | Difficulty: 4/5 | Would take again: No | Grade: B- | Tags: Tough grader
she literally never communicates and this class is impossible without it.

Professor: Jamie Garner | Department: Linguistics | School: University of Florida | Course: LIN3010 | Quality: 5/5 | Difficulty: 4/5 | Would take again: Yes | Grade: Not sure yet | Tags: Get ready to read, Accessible outside class, Online Savvy
I had trouble for 2 weeks this summer. Dr. Garner has been understanding and patient with my situation, which I really appreciate since other professors can fight against me when I have flare-ups (disability). Dr. Garner is accessible and creates great lectures with transcripts. The class is difficult and new for me, but I'm glad she's teaching it.
